Home›Smartphones›How to Force Restart an Android Phone

Smartphone how-to and troubleshooting

How to Force Restart an Android Phone

Force restart a frozen Android phone and account for manufacturer-specific button combinations safely.

Published: 2026-07-23Updated: 2026-07-23
Verification status
Byline
SolQvia.com Editorial Team
Review level
Official-source and procedure-impact review completed
Visuals
Original concept diagram, not a real-device screenshot
Reviewed
2026-07-23
In this guide
  1. Before you start
  2. Process diagram
  3. Steps
  4. Actions to avoid
  5. Prevention
  6. FAQ
Data impact: low

These steps normally do not erase stored data. Read any confirmation screen and app-specific notice.

Before you start

Do not begin with a broad reset. Check the following first so you can isolate the problem and avoid unnecessary data loss or reconfiguration.

  • A force restart is normally different from a data-erasing factory reset
  • Button combinations vary by manufacturer and model
  • If a boot menu appears, do not select an erase option

Critical diagnosis for this symptom

メーカー別の長押し時間を確認

Button combinations and hold times vary by manufacturer.

Before changing settings

Do not select unknown boot-menu options.

When to escalate

Escalate with device, OS, error, and reproduction details when the issue persists after restart, controlled comparison, and update checks, or when physical damage, abnormal heat, account suspension, or carrier provisioning is suspected.

Understand the process

Safe step-by-step procedure

Work from the top and stop as soon as the issue is resolved. Read every confirmation screen before moving to a higher-impact action.

  1. 1
    First hold the power button for about 30 seconds and see whether the device restarts.
  2. 2
    If it does not, check the manufacturer’s official model-specific procedure. Many phones use power plus volume down, but timing and behavior vary.
  3. 3
    Release the buttons when the logo appears and allow startup to finish.
  4. 4
    If a Recovery or Bootloader screen appears, do not choose Factory reset or Wipe data; choose Restart or Reboot system now.
  5. 5
    For repeated freezes, review storage, updates, problematic apps and overheating.

Actions to avoid

  • Using a button sequence meant for another model
  • Selecting Wipe data in Recovery
  • Holding buttons down with a tool

Prevention and preparation

  • Keep a record of manufacturer, model and model number
  • Check backup and free space before system updates
If the issue remains

Record whether the symptom occurs across apps, networks or accessories. Give official support the model, OS version, time, error text and steps already attempted.

Frequently asked questions

What if my screen looks different?

Labels and locations vary by OS, manufacturer, model and region. Use Settings search and check the manufacturer’s model-specific instructions.

What if I am unsure partway through?

Go back and confirm that no action has been committed. Do not proceed through an erase or account-change warning until you understand it.

What if the problem remains?

Test another connection or accessory where relevant, verify backups, then contact the manufacturer or carrier through official support.

This is general operational guidance. Screens and impact vary by OS, model, carrier and device management. Verify official instructions and backups before erasing data, changing accounts or arranging repair.

Official references

  • Android Help: Fix a device that freezes
  • Google Pixel Help: Restart a Pixel phone
